commits: ceffaad: docs(0.5.2): NAME-aliases implementation design memo
e5a7a80: test(0.5.2): RED — collision-safe alias resolution, shared classifier, catalog inference, batch-key + reference integrity
50d2920: feat(0.5.2): provider-prefixed model aliases + collision-safe resolution
68a49c0: fix(0.5.2): block ambiguous dropped bodies from fuzzy repoint (codex BLOCK)

tests.note: 1 xpassed — the pre-existing xfail tests/test_config_consistency.py::TestAliasTableCoverage::test_all_model_names_resolve_to_self (strict=False) now resolves correctly thanks to the collision-safe loader; reported as xpassed, not a failure.

blockers_encountered.description: [DETECT] Codex review found a real correctness bug: the two-pass loader correctly DROPS ambiguous variant keys (bodies claimed by >=2 entries under different providers) from _exact, but resolve() still leaked those dropped bodies into the fuzzy _score_match slow path. A NON-slash input like resolve('gemini-3.1-pro-preview') (served by BOTH gemini/ and vertex_ai/, no explicit alias equal to that body) was not in _exact, had no '/', fell to fuzzy, scored a ~1.0 exact-body match, and silently picked the FIRST entry (AI-Studio) and cached it — the exact silent cross-provider repoint the design said to prevent.
blockers_encountered.resolution: [RESOLVE] Added self._ambiguous_variants set (init in __init__, reset in load_from_config); pass 2 records every dropped multi-claimer variant key into it. resolve() now returns None (no fuzzy, no cache) for both the slash case (bare in _ambiguous_variants, checked after index/body/exact steps) and the non-slash case (lower in _ambiguous_variants, checked immediately before the fuzzy loop). Explicit-key matches still return at the top via _exact, so only genuinely-ambiguous dropped bodies are blocked. RED-first regression added in tests/test_model_alias.py::TestAmbiguousBodyNoFuzzyRepoint (synthetic two-provider config + real-config gemini-3.1-pro-preview); confirmed RED then GREEN. Fixed in commit 68a49c0. Verified: resolve('gemini-3.1-pro-preview') -> None (uncached) while resolve('aistudio/gemini-3.1-pro')/resolve('vertex/gemini-3.1-pro') still resolve to their own entries.

additional_changes_made_in_scope: Config anchors drifted as new entries were inserted (expected per brief); all edits were anchored on entry bodies, not line numbers, so no manual re-grep adjustment was needed.

NOTE for orchestrator: tests/test_config_consistency.py loads TWO configs — the existing TestAliasTableCoverage/TestFallbackConsistency suites read airlock/cli/templates/config.yaml (the simpler CLI template, NOT in this pack's MODIFY set, left untouched), while the NEW reference-integrity + batch-key tests read the deployed root config.yaml. The template is intentionally not updated here (out of scope); template-based tests still pass.

capability.py was kept minimal (airlock_provider_for + normalize_provider_token only). endpoints_for/capability_record/model_info shown in design note §4.5 are CAP-modelinfo's surface and were deliberately NOT added.
Post-review fix added self._ambiguous_variants to ModelAliasTable + two None-guards in resolve() (codex-BLOCK above).
agent_verify_result: pass — targeted: 251 passed, 1 xpassed, 0 failed. Adjacent regression after the fix (guardian/monitor/batch-gateway/enhanced): 135 passed. Pre-fix full suite was 2237 passed / 107 skipped / 1 xpassed with 1 pre-existing unrelated failure (tests/test_fathom_init.py::test_init_engine_with_fathomdb — fails by design because optional fathomdb is not installed; imports none of capability/model_alias/router).
next_step_for_orchestrator: Pack 0.5.2-NAME-aliases on branch feat/0.5.2-NAME-aliases at 68a49c0f9f395a59c404eb7b6b453450ce2b5724; review, verify, merge. Optional follow-up: mirror the new prefixed aliases into airlock/cli/templates/config.yaml if the CLI template should advertise them (out of scope here).
